decision.rationale: The multi-source retrieval direction is the better long-term fit because it preserves a single controlled retrieval core while keeping source-specific behavior isolated behind adapters.

purpose: Evolve codecompass/rag_helper from code-focused retrieval into a controlled multi-source retrieval core that supports repo/code content, artifacts/task memory and local offline wiki knowledge without collapsing source boundaries.
goal: A single retrieval core can index and retrieve repo/code content, artifacts/task memory and offline wiki knowledge with clear source separation, provenance, citations and hub-controlled orchestration.
non_goals: Do not build a full internet replacement.
Do not merge all source types into one untyped blob index.
Do not break current repo/code retrieval behavior for existing callers.
Do not introduce worker-to-worker orchestration or hidden background control loops.
Do not require cloud dependencies for the wiki MVP.
architecture_constraints: The hub remains the owner of planning, task routing, policy decisions, verification and queue control.
Workers execute delegated indexing, import, query and artifact-building tasks only.
Each hub and worker instance runs in its own container; no feature may assume implicit single-process shared state.
Additive changes are preferred over breaking refactors; compatibility adapters should be used where practical.
Retrieval sources must remain explicitly typed and traceable end-to-end.
Unsafe or incomplete source configuration must fail closed.
solid_focus: SRP: shared retrieval orchestration, source adapters, ranking, storage and API concerns remain separated.
OCP: new retrieval sources should be added by implementing interfaces and adapters rather than patching central logic repeatedly.
DIP: core retrieval services depend on source abstractions and policies, not wiki-specific or repo-specific concrete implementations.
success_criteria: codecompass remains stable for existing repo/code retrieval.
wiki content can be imported and queried through the same retrieval core.
results preserve source type and citation metadata.
mixed retrieval across repo, artifacts and wiki is possible but controlled.
the system stays understandable and does not collapse into one unstructured blob index.
import, indexing and query flows remain hub-controlled and auditable.

milestones.title: Architecture and compatibility baseline
milestones.target_outcome: Source model, interfaces, metadata, hub orchestration and regression safety are defined before deeper implementation.

milestones.title: Wiki MVP on shared core
milestones.target_outcome: A curated offline wiki corpus can be imported, indexed and queried through the multi-source core with citations.

milestones.title: Mixed retrieval and operator readiness
milestones.target_outcome: Mixed retrieval, diagnostics, configuration, API surface and demonstrations are stable and understandable.

tasks.title: Multi-source target model for codecompass define
acceptance_criteria: A short design note explains that codecompass evolves into a multi-source retrieval core.
Source classes are explicitly separated into at least repo, artifact/task and wiki.
The design states what remains shared and what stays source-specific.
The design explicitly preserves backward-compatible repo/code behavior during migration.
notes: Im Code existiert bereits ein faktischer Multi-Source-Kern: `repository_map`, `semantic_search`, `agentic_search`, `knowledge_index` und `result_memory` werden gemeinsam fusioniert.
Die Trennung zwischen shared orchestration und source-spezifischer Retrieval-Logik ist technisch angelegt, aber noch nicht als explizites CodeCompass-Target-Model dokumentiert.
Ein eigener Architektur- oder Design-Note fuer den angestrebten typed multi-source retrieval core fehlt noch.
implementation_notes: `agent/services/retrieval_service.py` zeigt bereits die gemeinsame Retrieval-Seam und Fusion mehrerer Quellen.
`agent/services/knowledge_index_retrieval_service.py` und `agent/routes/knowledge.py` behandeln artefakt-/knowledge-basierte Retrieval-Pfade als additive Quelle.
Die vorhandene Doku beschreibt Hybrid-RAG und Knowledge-Indexing, aber noch nicht sauber die neue Zielarchitektur `repo + artifact/task + wiki` als ein kontrolliertes Source-Modell.

tasks.title: Common source interface define
acceptance_criteria: A shared source interface or contract exists for all retrieval sources.
Interface covers import, chunk emission, metadata and stable source identifiers.
Existing repo/code path can be adapted without behavioral regression.
The interface is narrow enough that sources do not depend on methods they do not need.
notes: Es gibt bereits einen de-facto gemeinsamen Retrieval-Contract ueber `ContextChunk` und serialisierte Chunk-Payloads.
Dieser Contract deckt Chunk-Emission, Score, Source und Metadaten gut ab, aber Import-/Index-Lifecycle und stabile Source-Identifier sind noch nicht als eigenes Interface modelliert.
Die Quelle `knowledge_index` haengt noch ueber konkrete Service-Kopplung am Retrieval-Service statt ueber eine formale Source-Registry oder Port.
implementation_notes: `agent/services/retrieval_service.py` kann bestehende und additive Quellen ueber denselben Chunk-/Fusion-Pfad zusammenfuehren.
Fertig ist der Task erst, wenn daraus ein bewusst schmales Interface fuer import/index/search/metadata mit klaren Source-IDs wird.

tasks.title: Common document and chunk metadata model define
acceptance_criteria: A normalized metadata schema exists for all chunks.
Metadata includes source_type, source_id, chunk_id and provenance fields.
Wiki-specific metadata can be added without breaking repo/code metadata.
Metadata supports read-model summaries, diagnostics and later citation rendering.
notes: Ein Teil des Metadatenmodells ist schon da: `knowledge_index_id`, `artifact_id`, `collection_names`, `source_scope`, `record_kind`, `retrieval_intent` und Fusion-/Explainability-Felder.
Das Modell ist aber noch engine-zentriert statt source-zentriert; Felder wie `source_type`, `source_id` und ein stabiler universeller `chunk_id` fehlen als Pflichtschema.
Fuer spaetere Wiki-Integration ist das kritisch, weil sonst wieder source-spezifische Spezialfaelle im Core landen.
implementation_notes: `agent/services/knowledge_index_retrieval_service.py` erzeugt bereits reichhaltige Provenance-Metadaten fuer additive Retrieval-Quellen.
`agent/services/context_bundle_service.py` und die zugehoerigen Tests konsumieren Explainability- und Provenance-Felder bereits weiter.
Der Task ist erst abgeschlossen, wenn diese Felder in ein normales chunk/document metadata schema ueberfuehrt und fuer alle Quellen vereinheitlicht sind.

tasks.title: Hub-owned orchestration contract for import, index and query flows define
acceptance_criteria: The design states which workflow steps are hub-owned and which are worker-executed.
Import, reindex and retrieval flows do not introduce worker-to-worker orchestration.
Task payloads and state transitions are explicit enough to remain auditable across containers.
Failure handling and retries remain coordinated by the hub rather than embedded in source workers.
notes: Die HTTP-Einstiege fuer Upload, Collection-Indexing und Search laufen bereits hub-zentriert ueber Routen und Job-Services.
Asynchrone Laeufe sind observable und statusbehaftet, aber noch nicht als expliziter allgemeiner Hub-Orchestrierungsvertrag fuer alle Retrieval-Sources beschrieben.
Fuer die geplante Wiki-Quelle fehlt noch die klare Festlegung, welche Import-, Reindex- und Query-Schritte als Hub-Tasks materialisiert werden.
implementation_notes: `agent/routes/artifacts.py` und `agent/routes/knowledge.py` zeigen bereits additive API-Einstiege fuer Ingest, Index und Search.
`agent/services/knowledge_index_job_service.py` verschiebt laengere Index-Laeufe aus dem Request-Thread in beobachtbare Jobs, bleibt aber noch source-spezifisch.
Zum Abschluss braucht es einen source-uebergreifenden Orchestrierungsvertrag mit expliziten Task-States statt nur route-/service-spezifischen Pfaden.

tasks.title: Repo/code retrieval path isolate behind source adapter
acceptance_criteria: Current repo/code behavior is encapsulated as an explicit source adapter.
Existing code retrieval tests still pass.
The repo/code path no longer assumes it is the only retrieval source.
The adapter boundary reduces direct coupling between repo retrieval and shared orchestration.
notes: Der Repo-/Code-Pfad ist bereits weitgehend vom API-Layer entkoppelt und wird ueber `RetrievalService` plus `HybridOrchestrator` konsumiert.
Er ist aber noch kein expliziter Source-Adapter; `HybridOrchestrator` kapselt mehrere Engines zusammen und mischt damit Orchestrierung und konkrete Repo-Implementierung.
Regressionstests fuer das additive Verhalten gegen `knowledge_index` existieren bereits.
implementation_notes: `agent/services/retrieval_service.py` stellt den stabilen Retrieval-Seam fuer bestehende Caller bereit.
`agent/hybrid_orchestrator.py` kapselt `RepositoryMapEngine`, `SemanticSearchEngine` und `AgenticSearchEngine`, nimmt dem Repo-Pfad also schon die Alleinstellung.
Der Task ist fertig, wenn der Repo-/Code-Pfad explizit als typed source adapter hinter der shared core orchestration steht.

tasks.title: Artifact/task memory retrieval path isolate behind source adapter
acceptance_criteria: Artifacts/task memory can be treated as a distinct retrieval source.
Returned chunks preserve task/artifact provenance.
This source can be enabled or disabled independently.
Task-memory specific behavior does not leak into repo or wiki adapters.
notes: Artefakt- und Task-Memory sind bereits als additive Retrieval-Pfade sichtbar: `knowledge_index` fuer Artefaktwissen und `result_memory` fuer Worker-/Task-Ergebnisse.
Unabhaengiges Enabling/Disabling und eine wirklich saubere Adaptergrenze fehlen noch; `RetrievalService` kennt beide Quellen noch direkt.
implementation_notes: `agent/services/retrieval_service.py` baut `result_memory` als separaten Kandidatenstrom aus Task-/Goal-Nachbarschaft auf.

`agent/services/ingestion_service.py` und `agent/routes/knowledge.py` verankern den artefaktbezogenen Ingest-/Collection-Pfad bereits im System, aber noch nicht als allgemeines typed source adapter model.

tasks.title: Wiki source adapter design define
acceptance_criteria: A dedicated wiki source adapter is designed for local knowledge documents.
Wiki metadata includes article_title, section_title, language and import/revision metadata.
The adapter conforms to the shared source interface.
The design does not require wiki-specific branching in the shared retrieval core.

tasks.title: Offline wiki importer implement
acceptance_criteria: A local importer can read curated wiki content from one supported offline format.
Import failures are logged without corrupting the index.
Imported records are normalized into the shared document model.
The importer is deterministic for the same input corpus and configuration.

tasks.title: Wiki-aware chunker implement
acceptance_criteria: Wiki documents are chunked by article and section structure where possible.
Chunks remain small enough for retrieval but preserve context.
Chunk metadata supports later citations.
Chunk identifiers remain stable across rebuilds when source content is unchanged.

tasks.title: Shared indexing pipeline refactor
acceptance_criteria: The indexing pipeline works for multiple source types.
Indexes remain deterministic and rebuildable.
Source-specific fields do not break the shared index format.
The pipeline can be invoked by hub-owned tasks without assuming local mutable singleton state.

tasks.title: Per-source storage layout define
acceptance_criteria: Storage layout distinguishes repo/code, artifact and wiki content cleanly.
Reindexing one source type does not require destructive rebuild of all others unless explicitly requested.
Storage paths are configurable and laptop-friendly.
The layout works across container boundaries without hidden shared-state assumptions.

tasks.title: Shared retrieval service support source filters
acceptance_criteria: Queries can target repo-only, wiki-only, artifact-only or mixed retrieval.
Source filters are explicit in the query API.
Returned results preserve source origin.
Source selection behavior is visible in traces or diagnostics.

tasks.title: Hybrid ranking across sources design
acceptance_criteria: A ranking strategy exists for comparing chunks across different source types.
The design avoids repo code being drowned by wiki text or vice versa.
Per-source weighting is configurable.
Ranking policy can evolve independently of source adapters.

tasks.title: Citations and provenance format unify
acceptance_criteria: All retrieval results return unified provenance fields.
Wiki citations include article and section references.
Repo citations include file/path references.
Artifact citations include task/artifact references.

tasks.title: Configuration surface for source enabling add
acceptance_criteria: Config can enable or disable individual source types.
Wiki-specific config includes corpus path and indexing options.
Unsafe or broken source config fails closed.
Default config preserves current repo/code usage for existing deployments.

tasks.title: Preflight diagnostics for all source types add
acceptance_criteria: Preflight can report whether repo, artifact and wiki sources are available.
Diagnostics expose corpus/index presence and obvious problems.
Operators can understand why retrieval quality is degraded.
Diagnostics identify source-specific and orchestration-level failures separately.

tasks.title: API for source-aware retrieval extend
acceptance_criteria: Retrieval endpoints accept source selection or source policy parameters.
Responses contain source type and citation metadata.
Backward compatibility for existing callers is preserved where needed.
New API fields are additive and optional for legacy clients.

tasks.title: Context-bundle policy extend for multi-source retrieval
acceptance_criteria: Context assembly can budget chunks per source type.
Policies prevent one source from flooding the context window.
The effective retrieval mix is visible in diagnostics.
Policy decisions remain centralized and configurable rather than buried in adapters.

tasks.title: Wiki-only MVP corpus prepare
acceptance_criteria: A small curated local wiki corpus exists for initial validation.
Corpus is sufficient to prove end-to-end import, index and query.
MVP corpus is documented and reproducible.
Corpus scope is intentionally narrow and laptop-friendly.

tasks.title: Mixed retrieval demo define
acceptance_criteria: There is a demo where repo context and wiki context are both retrieved intentionally.
The demo shows why mixed retrieval is useful.
The output clearly distinguishes source origins.
The demo can be executed without depending on internet access.

tasks.title: Golden path for offline wiki-backed help document
acceptance_criteria: A first official workflow explains how to enable and use wiki-backed retrieval.
The flow is reproducible for another developer.
The flow includes citations in the result.
The documentation explains what the wiki source is for and what it is not for.

tasks.title: Regression tests for repo/code behavior add
acceptance_criteria: Existing codecompass behavior is protected by tests before refactor completion.
Repo retrieval quality does not silently regress.
Tests cover source separation assumptions.
Regression coverage exists before deeper shared-core refactors are merged.

tasks.title: Importer, indexer and retriever tests for wiki source add
acceptance_criteria: Wiki importer has basic tests.
Wiki chunking/indexing has deterministic tests.
Wiki retrieval returns stable source metadata.
Tests cover malformed inputs and deterministic rebuild behavior.

tasks.title: Smoke test for end-to-end multi-source retrieval add
acceptance_criteria: A smoke test covers import/index/query across at least repo and wiki sources.
Returned citations are validated.
The system proves source separation under mixed retrieval.
The smoke test can run in a reproducible local/containerized environment.

tasks.title: Read-model output include source summaries
acceptance_criteria: Assistant/dashboard summaries can show which source classes contributed.
Source contributions are understandable at a glance.
Admin views retain deeper provenance detail.
Non-admin views do not expose more provenance detail than intended.

tasks.title: Performance and storage limits for laptop setups document
acceptance_criteria: There is a documented small-scale recommended corpus size for laptops.
Storage and indexing costs are roughly described.
The initial rollout avoids trying to ingest too much at once.
Observed limits are tied to a concrete reproducible MVP corpus.

tasks.title: Redaction and provenance policy for wiki and mixed-source output add
acceptance_criteria: Wiki and mixed-source output follow explicit provenance rules.
Stored and displayed metadata are appropriate for admin and non-admin views.
No hidden source blending occurs in rendered results or context bundles.
Policy application is testable and observable.

tasks.title: Hub-triggered API endpoints for import, reindex and query flows add
acceptance_criteria: There are API entry points for import, reindex and retrieval flows.
APIs trigger hub-owned workflows rather than bypassing the orchestration model.
Responses expose traceability, source information and workflow status where appropriate.
The API design remains additive for existing clients.

tasks.title: Container and runtime assumptions document
acceptance_criteria: Runtime assumptions for hub, workers, storage and index access are written down explicitly.
The design identifies what must be persisted across container restarts.
No workflow step depends on accidental in-memory state sharing between containers.
Operational prerequisites are concrete enough for local and CI environments.

tasks.title: Future source roadmap prepare
acceptance_criteria: The design leaves room for future FreeCAD, KiCad, Blender or handbook sources.
Roadmap does not require immediate implementation.
Source extensibility is documented without overcomplicating the MVP.
Future source examples validate that the current abstractions are not wiki-specific.
